evidence: pyPDF2 declared in cowabunga_evals/pyproject.toml but never imported in any source file
seaborn declared in cowabunga_evals/pyproject.toml but never imported in any source file
python-dotenv appears twice in cowabunga_evals/pyproject.toml dependencies array
transformers version conflict: root pyproject.toml dev-whisper specifies 4.45.1, packages/whisper/pyproject.toml dev specifies 4.39.3
toml and yaml (PyYAML) imported directly in src/cowabunga_api/utils/config.py but not declared in cowabunga_api/pyproject.toml
Both dompurify and isomorphic-dompurify in UI production dependencies; Message.svelte uses dompurify while CodeBlock.js uses isomorphic-dompurify for same HTML sanitization purpose
aiostream declared in packages/vllm/pyproject.toml but never imported in any vllm source file
dependency_health.issues_preventing_higher_score: Multiple unused or duplicate dependencies, undeclared direct imports, and a version conflict across packages indicate dependency manifests have drifted from actual usage.

dependency_health.dimension_character: The dependency surface is mostly well-organized across a multi-package monorepo with explicit version pinning, but several manifests have drifted from actual usage. Unused deps, duplicate declarations, and undeclared direct imports suggest insufficient pruning of dependency lists.
dependency_health.score_rationale: The codebase avoids circular dependencies and uses a sensible monorepo structure, but the accumulation of unused deps (pyPDF2, seaborn, aiostream), duplicate declarations (python-dotenv, dompurify/isomorphic-dompurify), undeclared direct imports (toml, yaml), and a version conflict (transformers) creates regular friction. This places it in the solid-but-uneven range.

issues.summary: pyPDF2 declared but never used in cowabunga_evals
evidence: pyPDF2 == 3.0.1 is listed in dependencies but grep finds zero imports across the entire codebase

issues.summary: seaborn declared but never used in cowabunga_evals
evidence: seaborn == 0.13.2 is listed in dependencies but grep finds zero imports across the entire codebase

issues.summary: python-dotenv declared twice in same pyproject.toml
evidence: python-dotenv == 1.0.1 appears on both line 13 and line 20 of the dependencies array

issues.summary: toml and PyYAML imported directly but not declared in cowabunga_api dependencies
evidence: config.py imports toml and yaml directly, but neither package appears in cowabunga_api/pyproject.toml dependencies; they only work via transitive inclusion
issues.suggestion: Add toml and PyYAML as explicit dependencies in cowabunga_api/pyproject.toml

issues.summary: transformers version conflict between root and whisper package
evidence: Root pyproject.toml dev-whisper specifies transformers[torch] == 4.45.1, while packages/whisper/pyproject.toml dev specifies transformers[torch] == 4.39.3
issues.suggestion: Align transformers version across both manifests, or document why different versions are required

issues.summary: Both dompurify and isomorphic-dompurify in UI production dependencies
evidence: dompurify and isomorphic-dompurify both listed in UI dependencies; Message.svelte imports dompurify while CodeBlock.js imports isomorphic-dompurify for the same HTML sanitization purpose
issues.suggestion: Consolidate on isomorphic-dompurify for both server and browser contexts, remove dompurify

issues.summary: aiostream declared but never used in vllm package
evidence: aiostream ==0.6.2 is listed in dependencies but no vllm source file imports it

root_causes: Dependency manifests are not regularly audited against actual imports, leading to accumulated unused and duplicate declarations
Multi-package monorepo with separate pyproject.toml files creates opportunity for version drift when optional dependencies are pinned independently

add.description: Several packages (nltk, pylibmagic, python-magic, storage3, postgrest) are intentionally pinned as direct dependencies even though they are transitive, with explanatory comments documenting why. This is a healthy pattern that prevents upstream breakage.

add.header: No circular dependency cycles in Python import graph
add.description: A structural scan of the cowabunga_api and related source packages found no circular import cycles, indicating the module graph is clean.
toml and PyYAML are imported directly in config.py but are not listed in cowabunga_api/pyproject.toml; they happen to be available through transitive dependencies, making the build fragile.

add.header: Duplicate HTML sanitization libraries in UI
add.description: The UI package depends on both dompurify and isomorphic-dompurify, which serve the same purpose. CodeBlock.js uses isomorphic-dompurify while Message.svelte uses dompurify, suggesting inconsistent adoption.
